classes.name: AngularVelocityVector
classes.comment: 5.2.2: angular velocity measured in radians per second out each of the entity's own coordinate axes.

classes.name: UnsignedIntegerWrapper
classes.comment: Wrapper for an unsigned 32 bit integer

fields.name: wrapper
fields.comment: name can't be too accurate or the generated source code will have reserved word problems

classes.name: AntennaLocation
classes.comment: 5.2.3: location of the radiating portion of the antenna, specified in world coordinates and entity coordinates.

fields.name: antennaLocation
fields.comment: Location of the radiating portion of the antenna in world    coordinates

fields.name: relativeAntennaLocation
fields.comment: Location of the radiating portion of the antenna     in entity coordinates

classes.name: BeamAntennaPattern
classes.comment: Section 5.2.4.2. Used when the antenna pattern type field has a value of 1. Specifies           the direction, patter, and polarization of radiation from an antenna.

fields.name: beamDirection
fields.comment: The rotation that transformst he reference coordinate sytem     into the beam coordinate system. Either world coordinates or entity coordinates may be used as the     reference coordinate system, as specified by teh reference system field of the antenna pattern record.

fields.name: azimuthBeamwidth
fields.comment: Full width of the beam to the -3dB power density points in the x-y plane of the beam coordinnate system.  Elevation beamwidth is represented by a 32 bit floating point number in units of radians.

fields.name: elevationBeamwidth
fields.comment: The full width of the beam to the –3 dB power density points in the x-z plane of the beam coordinate system. Elevation beamwidth shall be represented by a 32-bit floating point number in units of radians.

fields.name: referenceSystem
fields.comment: The reference coordinate system wrt which beam direction      is specified. This field should not change over the duration of an exercise. World coordindate systemis      prefered for exercises. The entity coordinate system should be used only when highly directional antennas must be precisely modeled.

fields.name: ez
fields.comment: Magnigute of the z-component in beam coordinates at some arbitrary      single point in the mainbeam      and in the far field of the antenna.

fields.name: ex
fields.comment: Magnigute of the x-component in beam coordinates at some arbitrary      single point in the mainbeam      and in the far field of the antenna.

fields.name: phase
fields.comment: THe phase angle between Ez and Ex in radians.

classes.name: SphericalHarmonicAntennaPattern
classes.comment: Section 5.2.4.3. Used when the antenna pattern type in the transmitter pdu is of value 2.         Specified the direction and radiation pattern from a radio transmitter's antenna.        NOTE: this class must be hand-coded to clean up some implementation details.

fields.name: harmonicOrder
fields.comment: The highest order of the expansion in spherical harmonics, counting     from zero. There are 2n+1 coefficients for each order n, and a total of N^2 + 2N + 1 coefficients     through order N. note that order by itself is a sql reserved word

classes.name: ArticulationParameter
classes.comment: Section 5.2.5. Articulation parameters for  movable parts and attached parts of an entity. Specifes wether or not a change has occured,  the part identifcation of the articulated part to which it is attached, and the type and value of each parameter.

classes.name: BurstDescriptor
classes.comment: Section 5.2.7. Specifies the type of muntion fired, the type of warhead, the         type of fuse, the number of rounds fired, and the rate at which the roudns are fired in         rounds per minute.

fields.name: munition
fields.comment: What munition was used in the burst

fields.name: quantity
fields.comment: how many of the munition were fired

fields.name: rate
fields.comment: rate at which the munition was fired

classes.name: ClockTime
classes.comment: Section 5.2.8. Time measurements that exceed one hour. Hours is the number of           hours since January 1, 1970, UTC

classes.name: EmitterSystem
classes.comment: Section 5.2.11. This field shall specify information about a particular emitter system

fields.name: emitterName
fields.comment: Name of the emitter, 16 bit enumeration

fields.name: function
fields.comment: function of the emitter, 8 bit enumeration

classes.name: SimulationAddress
classes.comment: Section 5.2.14.1. A Simulation Address  record shall consist of the Site Identification number and the Application Identification number.

classes.name: EntityType
classes.comment: Section 5.2.16. Identifies the type of entity, including kind of entity, domain (surface, subsurface, air, etc) country, category, etc.

fields.name: domain
fields.comment: Domain of entity (air, surface, subsurface, space, etc)

fields.name: country
fields.comment: country to which the design of the entity is attributed

fields.name: spec
fields.comment: specific info based on subcategory field. Renamed from specific because that is a reserved word in SQL

classes.name: ObjectType
classes.comment: Identifies type of object. This is a shorter version of EntityType that omits the specific and extra fields.

fields.name: domain
fields.comment: Domain of entity (air, surface, subsurface, space, etc)

fields.name: country
fields.comment: country to which the design of the entity is attributed

classes.name: Orientation
classes.comment: Section 5.2.17. Three floating point values representing an orientation, psi, theta, and phi, aka the euler angles, in radians

classes.name: EventID
classes.comment: Section 5.2.18. Identifies a unique event in a simulation via the combination of three values

classes.name: FixedDatum
classes.comment: Section 5.2.18. Fixed Datum Record

classes.name: FundamentalParameterData
classes.comment: Section 5.2.22. Contains electromagnetic emmision regineratin parameters that are        variable throughout a scenario dependent on the actions of the participants in the simulation. Also provides basic parametric data that may be used to support low-fidelity simulations.

fields.name: frequency
fields.comment: center frequency of the emission in hertz.

fields.name: frequencyRange
fields.comment: Bandwidth of the frequencies corresponding to the fequency field.

fields.name: effectiveRadiatedPower
fields.comment: Effective radiated power for the emission in DdBm. For a      radar noise jammer, indicates the peak of the transmitted power.

fields.name: pulseRepetitionFrequency
fields.comment: Average repetition frequency of the emission in hertz.

fields.name: pulseWidth
fields.comment: Average pulse width  of the emission in microseconds.

fields.name: beamAzimuthCenter
fields.comment: Specifies the beam azimuth an elevation centers and corresponding half-angles     to describe the scan volume

fields.name: beamAzimuthSweep
fields.comment: Specifies the beam azimuth sweep to determine scan volume

fields.name: beamElevationCenter
fields.comment: Specifies the beam elevation center to determine scan volume

fields.name: beamElevationSweep
fields.comment: Specifies the beam elevation sweep to determine scan volume

fields.name: beamSweepSync
fields.comment: allows receiver to synchronize its regenerated scan pattern to     that of the emmitter. Specifies the percentage of time a scan is through its pattern from its origion.

classes.name: RadioEntityType
classes.comment: Section 5.2.25. Identifies the type of radio

fields.name: domain
fields.comment: Domain of entity (air, surface, subsurface, space, etc)

fields.name: country
fields.comment: country to which the design of the entity is attributed

fields.name: nomenclatureVersion
fields.comment: specific info based on subcategory field

classes.name: SupplyQuantity
classes.comment: Section 5.2.30. A supply, and the amount of that supply. Similar to an entity kind but with the addition of a quantity.

classes.name: VariableDatum
classes.comment: Section 5.2.32. Variable Datum Record

fields.name: variableDatumLength
fields.comment: length of the variable datums, in bits. Note that this is not programmatically tied to the size of the variableData. The variable data field may be 64 bits long but only 16 bits of it could actually be used.

fields.name: variableData
fields.comment: data can be any length, but must increase in 8 byte quanta. This requires some postprocessing patches. Note that setting the data allocates a new internal array to account for the possibly increased size. The default initial size is 64 bits.

classes.name: AcousticEmitter
classes.comment: Section 5.2.35. information about a specific UA emmtter

fields.name: acousticName
fields.comment: the system for a particular UA emitter, and an enumeration

fields.name: function
fields.comment: The function of the acoustic system

fields.name: acousticIdNumber
fields.comment: The UA emitter identification number relative to a specific system

classes.name: TrackJamTarget
classes.comment: One track/jam target

classes.name: ElectronicEmissionBeamData
classes.comment: Description of one electronic emission beam

fields.name: beamDataLength
fields.comment: This field shall specify the length of this beams data in 32 bit words

fields.name: beamIDNumber
fields.comment: This field shall specify a unique emitter database number assigned to differentiate between otherwise similar or identical emitter beams within an emitter system.

fields.name: beamParameterIndex
fields.comment: This field shall specify a Beam Parameter Index number that shall be used by receiving entities in conjunction with the Emitter Name field to provide a pointer to the stored database parameters required to regenerate the beam.

fields.name: fundamentalParameterData
fields.comment: Fundamental parameter data such as frequency range, beam sweep, etc.

fields.name: beamFunction
fields.comment: beam function of a particular beam

fields.name: highDensityTrackJam
fields.comment: wheher or not the receiving simulation apps can assume all the targets in the scan pattern are being tracked/jammed

fields.name: trackJamTargets
fields.comment: variable length variablelist of track/jam targets

classes.name: ElectronicEmissionSystemData
classes.comment: Data about one electronic system

fields.name: systemDataLength
fields.comment: This field shall specify the length of this emitter system�s data (including beam data and its track/jam information) in 32-bit words. The length shall include the System Data Length field.

fields.name: numberOfBeams
fields.comment: This field shall specify the number of beams being described in the current PDU for the system being described.

fields.name: emitterSystem
fields.comment: This field shall specify information about a particular emitter system

fields.name: location
fields.comment: Location with respect to the entity

fields.name: beamDataRecords
fields.comment: variable length variablelist of beam data records

classes.name: AggregateID
classes.comment: Section 5.2.36. Each agregate in a given simulation app is given an aggregate identifier number unique for all other aggregates in that app and in that exercise. The id is valid for the duration of the the exercise.

classes.name: Marking
classes.comment: Section 5.2.15. Specifies the character set used inthe first byte, followed by 11 characters of text data.

classes.name: AggregateMarking
classes.comment: Section 5.2.37. Specifies the character set used inthe first byte, followed by up to 31 characters of text data.

classes.name: AggregateType
classes.comment: Section 5.2.38. Identifies the type of aggregate including kind of entity, domain (surface, subsurface, air, etc) country, category, etc.

fields.name: domain
fields.comment: Domain of entity (air, surface, subsurface, space, etc)

fields.name: country
fields.comment: country to which the design of the entity is attributed

fields.name: specificInfo
fields.comment: specific info based on subcategory field, sql has a reserved word for specific

classes.name: BeamData
classes.comment: Section 5.2.39. Specification of the data necessary to  describe the scan volume of an emitter.

fields.name: beamAzimuthCenter
fields.comment: Specifies the beam azimuth an elevation centers and corresponding half-angles     to describe the scan volume

fields.name: beamAzimuthSweep
fields.comment: Specifies the beam azimuth sweep to determine scan volume

classes.name: Environment
classes.comment: Section 5.2.40. Information about a geometry, a state associated with a geometry, a bounding volume, or an associated entity ID. NOTE: this class requires hand coding.

fields.name: recordIndex
fields.comment: Identify the sequentially numbered record index

fields.name: padding2
fields.comment: padding to bring the total size up to a 64 bit boundry

classes.name: Vector3Float
classes.comment: Section 5.2.33. Three floating point values, x, y, and z

classes.name: Vector3Double
classes.comment: Section 5.3.34. Three double precision floating point values, x, y, and z

classes.name: EntityID
classes.comment: Each entity in a given DIS simulation application shall be given an entity identifier number unique to all  other entities in that application. This identifier number is valid for the duration of the exercise; however,  entity identifier numbers may be reused when all possible numbers have been exhausted. No entity shall  have an entity identifier number of NO_ENTITY, ALL_ENTITIES, or RQST_ASSIGN_ID. The entity iden-  tifier number need not be registered or retained for future exercises. The entity identifier number shall be  specified by a 16-bit unsigned integer.  An entity identifier number equal to zero with valid site and application identification shall address a  simulation application. An entity identifier number equal to ALL_ENTITIES shall mean all entities within  the specified site and application. An entity identifier number equal to RQST_ASSIGN_ID allows the  receiver of the create entity to define the entity identifier number of the new entity.

classes.name: ShaftRPMs
classes.comment: Shaft RPMs, used in underwater acoustic clacluations.

classes.name: AcousticEmitterSystem
classes.comment: 5.3.35: Information about a particular UA emitter shall be represented using an Acoustic Emitter System record. This record shall consist of three fields: Acoustic Name, Function, and Acoustic ID Number

fields.name: acousticName
fields.comment: This field shall specify the system for a particular UA emitter.

fields.name: acousticFunction
fields.comment: This field shall describe the function of the acoustic system.

fields.name: acousticID
fields.comment: This field shall specify the UA emitter identification number relative to a specific system. This field shall be represented by an 8-bit unsigned integer. This field allows the differentiation of multiple systems on an entity, even if in some instances two or more of the systems may be identical UA emitter types. Numbering of systems shall begin with the value 1.

classes.name: AcousticEmitterSystemData
classes.comment: Used in the UA pdu; ties together an emmitter and a location. This requires manual cleanup; the beam data should not be attached to each emitter system.

fields.name: acousticEmitterSystem
fields.comment: This field shall specify the system for a particular UA emitter.

fields.comment: Represents the location wrt the entity

fields.name: beamRecords
fields.comment: For each beam in numberOfBeams, an emitter system. This is not right--the beam records need to be at the end of the PDU, rather than attached to each system.

fields.comment: variable length list of fixed datums

fields.comment: variable length list of variable length datums

classes.name: EventReportPdu
classes.comment: Section 5.3.6.11. Reports occurance of a significant event to the simulation manager. COMPLETE

fields.name: fixedDatums
fields.comment: variable length list of fixed datums

fields.name: variableDatums
fields.comment: variable length list of variable length datums

classes.name: CommentPdu
classes.comment: Section 5.3.6.12. Arbitrary messages can be entered into the data stream via use of this PDU. COMPLETE

fields.name: fixedDatums
fields.comment: variable length list of fixed datums

fields.name: variableDatums
fields.comment: variable length list of variable length datums

classes.name: DistributedEmissionsFamilyPdu
classes.comment: Section 5.3.7. Electronic Emissions. Abstract superclass for distirubted emissions PDU

classes.name: ElectronicEmissionsPdu
classes.comment: Section 5.3.7.1. Information about active electronic warfare (EW) emissions and active EW countermeasures shall be communicated using an Electromagnetic Emission PDU. COMPLETE (I think)

fields.name: stateUpdateIndicator
fields.comment: This field shall be used to indicate if the data in the PDU represents a state update or just data that has changed since issuance of the last Electromagnetic Emission PDU [relative to the identified entity and emission system(s)].

fields.name: numberOfSystems
fields.comment: This field shall specify the number of emission systems being described in the current PDU.

classes.name: DesignatorPdu
classes.comment: Section 5.3.7.2. Handles designating operations. COMPLETE

fields.name: codeName
fields.comment: This field shall specify a unique emitter database number assigned to  differentiate between otherwise similar or identical emitter beams within an emitter system.

fields.name: designatedEntityID
fields.comment: ID of the entity being designated

fields.name: designatorCode
fields.comment: This field shall identify the designator code being used by the designating entity

fields.name: designatorPower
fields.comment: This field shall identify the designator output power in watts

fields.name: designatorWavelength
fields.comment: This field shall identify the designator wavelength in units of microns

fields.name: designatorSpotWrtDesignated
fields.comment: designtor spot wrt the designated entity

fields.name: designatorSpotLocation
fields.comment: designtor spot wrt the designated entity

classes.name: UaPdu
classes.comment: Section 5.3.7.3. Information about underwater acoustic emmissions. This requires manual cleanup.  The beam data records should ALL be a the finish, rather than attached to each emitter system. UNFINISHED

fields.name: emittingEntityID
fields.comment: ID of the entity that is the source of the emission

fields.name: stateChangeIndicator
fields.comment: This field shall be used to indicate whether the data in the UA PDU represent a state update or data that have changed since issuance of the last UA PDU

fields.name: passiveParameterIndex
fields.comment: This field indicates which database record (or file) shall be used in the definition of passive signature (unintentional) emissions of the entity. The indicated database record (or  file) shall define all noise generated as a function of propulsion plant configurations and associated  auxiliaries.

fields.name: propulsionPlantConfiguration
fields.comment: This field shall specify the entity propulsion plant configuration. This field is used to determine the passive signature characteristics of an entity.

fields.name: numberOfShafts
fields.comment: This field shall represent the number of shafts on a platform

fields.name: numberOfAPAs
fields.comment: This field shall indicate the number of APAs described in the current UA PDU

fields.name: numberOfUAEmitterSystems
fields.comment: This field shall specify the number of UA emitter systems being described in the current UA PDU

classes.name: IffAtcNavAidsLayer1Pdu
classes.comment: 5.3.7.4.1: Navigational and IFF PDU. COMPLETE

fields.name: emittingEntityId
fields.comment: ID of the entity that is the source of the emissions

fields.name: eventID
fields.comment: Number generated by the issuing simulation to associate realted events.

fields.name: location
fields.comment: Location wrt entity. There is some ambugiuity in the standard here, but this is the order it is listed in the table.

classes.name: IffAtcNavAidsLayer2Pdu
classes.comment: Section 5.3.7.4.2 When present, layer 2 should follow layer 1 and have the following fields. This requires manual cleanup.        the beamData attribute semantics are used in multiple ways. UNFINSISHED

fields.name: fundamentalIffParameters
fields.comment: variable length list of fundamental parameters. ^^^This is wrong

classes.name: SeesPdu
classes.comment: Section 5.3.7.5. SEES PDU, supplemental emissions entity state information. COMPLETE

fields.name: propulsionSystemData
fields.comment: variable length list of propulsion system data

fields.name: vectoringSystemData
fields.comment: variable length list of vectoring system data

classes.name: RadioCommunicationsFamilyPdu
classes.comment: Section 5.3.8. Abstract superclass for radio communications PDUs.

classes.name: IntercomSignalPdu
classes.comment: Section 5.3.8.4. Actual transmission of intercome voice data. COMPLETE

fields.name: entityId
fields.comment: ID of the entitythat is the source of the communication

classes.name: TransmitterPdu
classes.comment: Section 5.3.8.1. Detailed information about a radio transmitter. This PDU requires manually         written code to complete, since the modulation parameters are of variable length. UNFINISHED

fields.name: entityId
fields.comment: ID of the entity that is the source of the communication, ie contains the radio

fields.name: relativeAntennaLocation
fields.comment: relative location of antenna, in entity coordinates

fields.name: modulationParameterCount
fields.comment: length of modulation parameters in octets

fields.name: modulationParametersList
fields.comment: variable length list of modulation parameters

fields.name: antennaPatternList
fields.comment: variable length list of antenna pattern records

classes.name: SignalPdu
classes.comment: Section 5.3.8.2. Detailed information about a radio transmitter. This PDU requires manually written code to complete. The encodingScheme field can be used in multiple ways, which requires hand-written code to finish. UNFINISHED

fields.name: entityId
fields.comment: ID of the entity that is the source of the communication, ie contains the radio

fields.name: samples
fields.comment: number of samples. If the PDU contains encoded audio, this should be zero.

fields.name: data
fields.comment: list of eight bit values. Must be padded to fall on a 32 bit boundary.

classes.name: ReceiverPdu
classes.comment: Section 5.3.8.3. Communication of a receiver state. COMPLETE

fields.name: entityId
fields.comment: ID of the entity that is the source of the communication, ie contains the radio

classes.name: IntercomControlPdu
classes.comment: Section 5.3.8.5. Detailed inofrmation about the state of an intercom device and the actions it is requestion         of another intercom device, or the response to a requested action. Required manual intervention to fix the intercom parameters,        which can be of varialbe length. UNFINSISHED

fields.name: sourceCommunicationsDeviceID
fields.comment: The specific intercom device being simulated within an entity.

fields.name: sourceLineID
fields.comment: Line number to which the intercom control refers

fields.name: transmitPriority
fields.comment: priority of this message relative to transmissons from other intercom devices

fields.name: transmitLineState
fields.comment: current transmit state of the line

fields.name: masterEntityID
fields.comment: eid of the entity that has created this intercom channel.

fields.name: masterCommunicationsDeviceID
fields.comment: specific intercom device that has created this intercom channel

classes.name: EntityManagementFamilyPdu
classes.comment: Section 5.3.9. Common superclass for EntityManagment PDUs, including aggregate state, isGroupOf, TransferControLRequest, and isPartOf

classes.name: AggregateStatePdu
classes.comment: Section 5.3.9.1 informationa bout aggregating entities anc communicating information about the aggregated entities.        requires manual intervention to fix the padding between entityID lists and silent aggregate sysem lists--this padding        is dependent on how many entityIDs there are, and needs to be on a 32 bit word boundary. UNFINISHED

fields.name: aggregateType
fields.comment: entity type of the aggregated entities

fields.name: aggregateMarking
fields.comment: marking for aggregate; first char is charset type, rest is char data

fields.name: dimensions
fields.comment: dimensions of bounding box for the aggregated entities, origin at the center of mass

fields.name: centerOfMass
fields.comment: center of mass of the aggregation

fields.name: pad2
fields.comment: ^^^padding to put the start of the next list on a 32 bit boundary. This needs to be fixed

classes.name: IsGroupOfPdu
classes.comment: Section 5.3.9.2 Information about a particular group of entities grouped together for the purposes of netowrk bandwidth         reduction or aggregation. Needs manual cleanup. The GED size requires a database lookup. UNFINISHED

fields.name: groupedEntityCategory
fields.comment: type of entities constituting the group

fields.name: numberOfGroupedEntities
fields.comment: Number of individual entities constituting the group

fields.name: groupedEntityDescriptions
fields.comment: GED records about each individual entity in the group. ^^^this is wrong--need a database lookup to find the actual size of the list elements

classes.name: TransferControlRequestPdu
classes.comment: Section 5.3.9.3 Information initiating the dyanic allocation and control of simulation entities         between two simulation applications. Requires manual cleanup. The padding between record sets is variable. UNFINISHED

fields.name: transferEntityID
fields.comment: The entity for which control is being requested to transfer

fields.name: numberOfRecordSets
fields.comment: number of record sets to transfer

fields.name: recordSets
fields.comment: ^^^This is wrong--the RecordSet class needs more work

classes.name: IsPartOfPdu
classes.comment: Section 5.3.9.4 The joining of two or more simulation entities is communicated by this PDU. COMPLETE

fields.name: partLocation
fields.comment: location of part; centroid of part in host's coordinate system. x=range, y=bearing, z=0

classes.name: MinefieldFamilyPdu
classes.comment: Section 5.3.10.1 Abstract superclass for PDUs relating to minefields

classes.name: MinefieldStatePdu
classes.comment: Section 5.3.10.1 Abstract superclass for PDUs relating to minefields. COMPLETE

fields.name: minefieldLocation
fields.comment: location of minefield in world coords

classes.name: MinefieldQueryPdu
classes.comment: Section 5.3.10.2 Query a minefield for information about individual mines. Requires manual clean up to get the padding right. UNFINISHED

fields.name: requestingEntityID
fields.comment: EID of entity making the request

fields.name: numberOfPerimeterPoints
fields.comment: Number of perimeter points for the minefield

fields.name: requestedMineType
fields.comment: Entity type of mine being requested

classes.name: MinefieldDataPdu
classes.comment: Section 5.3.10.3 Information about individual mines within a minefield. This is very, very wrong. UNFINISHED

fields.name: numberOfMinesInThisPdu
fields.comment: how many mines are in this PDU

fields.name: numberOfSensorTypes
fields.comment: how many sensor type are in this PDU

fields.name: pad3
fields.comment: Padding to get things 32-bit aligned. ^^^this is wrong--dyanmically sized padding needed

classes.name: MinefieldResponseNackPdu
classes.comment: Section 5.3.10.4 proivde the means to request a retransmit of a minefield data pdu. COMPLETE

fields.name: missingPduSequenceNumbers
fields.comment: PDU sequence numbers that were missing

classes.name: SyntheticEnvironmentFamilyPdu
classes.comment: Section 5.3.11: Abstract superclass for synthetic environment PDUs

classes.name: EnvironmentalProcessPdu
classes.comment: Section 5.3.11.1: Information about environmental effects and processes. This requires manual cleanup. the environmental        record is variable, as is the padding. UNFINISHED

fields.name: sequenceNumber
fields.comment: PDU sequence number for the environmentla process if pdu sequencing required

classes.name: GriddedDataPdu
classes.comment: Section 5.3.11.2: Information about globat, spatially varying enviornmental effects. This requires manual cleanup; the grid axis        records are variable sized. UNFINISHED

fields.name: fieldNumber
fields.comment: unique identifier for each piece of enviornmental data

fields.name: pduNumber
fields.comment: sequence number for the total set of PDUS used to transmit the data

fields.name: pduTotal
fields.comment: Total number of PDUS used to transmit the data

fields.name: numberOfGridAxes
fields.comment: number of grid axes for the environmental data

fields.name: constantGrid
fields.comment: are domain grid axes identidal to those of the priveious domain update?

fields.name: sampleTime
fields.comment: valid time of the enviormental data sample, 64 bit unsigned int

fields.name: totalValues
fields.comment: total number of all data values for all pdus for an environmental sample

fields.name: vectorDimension
fields.comment: total number of data values at each grid point.

classes.name: PointObjectStatePdu
classes.comment: Section 5.3.11.3: Inormation abut the addition or modification of a synthecic enviroment object that is anchored      to the terrain with a single point. COMPLETE

fields.name: referencedObjectID
fields.comment: Object with which this point object is associated

fields.name: updateNumber
fields.comment: unique update number of each state transition of an object

classes.name: LinearObjectStatePdu
classes.comment: Section 5.3.11.4: Information abut the addition or modification of a synthecic enviroment object that      is anchored to the terrain with a single point and has size or orientation. COMPLETE

fields.name: referencedObjectID
fields.comment: Object with which this point object is associated

fields.name: updateNumber
fields.comment: unique update number of each state transition of an object

classes.name: ArealObjectStatePdu
classes.comment: Section 5.3.11.5: Information about the addition/modification of an oobject that is geometrically      achored to the terrain with a set of three or more points that come to a closure. COMPLETE

fields.name: referencedObjectID
fields.comment: Object with which this point object is associated

fields.name: updateNumber
fields.comment: unique update number of each state transition of an object

classes.name: SimulationManagementWithReliabilityFamilyPdu
classes.comment: Section 5.3.12: Abstract superclass for reliable simulation management PDUs

fields.name: receivingEntityID
fields.comment: Object with which this point object is associated

classes.name: CreateEntityReliablePdu
classes.comment: Section 5.3.12.1: creation of an entity , reliable. COMPLETE

fields.name: requiredReliabilityService
fields.comment: level of reliability service used for this transaction

classes.name: RemoveEntityReliablePdu
classes.comment: Section 5.3.12.2: Removal of an entity , reliable. COMPLETE

fields.name: requiredReliabilityService
fields.comment: level of reliability service used for this transaction

classes.name: StartResumeReliablePdu
classes.comment: Section 5.3.12.3: Start resume simulation, relaible. COMPLETE

fields.name: realWorldTime
fields.comment: time in real world for this operation to happen

fields.name: simulationTime
fields.comment: time in simulation for the simulation to resume

fields.name: requiredReliabilityService
fields.comment: level of reliability service used for this transaction

classes.name: StopFreezeReliablePdu
classes.comment: Section 5.3.12.4: Stop freeze simulation, relaible. COMPLETE

fields.name: realWorldTime
fields.comment: time in real world for this operation to happen

fields.name: frozenBehavior
fields.comment: internal behvior of the simulation while frozen

classes.name: AcknowledgeReliablePdu
classes.comment: Section 5.3.12.5: Ack receipt of a start-resume, stop-freeze, create-entity or remove enitty (reliable) pdus. COMPLETE

classes.name: ActionRequestReliablePdu
classes.comment: Section 5.3.12.6: request from a simulation manager to a managed entity to perform a specified action. COMPLETE

fields.name: requiredReliabilityService
fields.comment: level of reliability service used for this transaction

classes.name: ActionResponseReliablePdu
classes.comment: Section 5.3.12.7: Response from an entity to an action request PDU. COMPLETE

classes.name: DataQueryReliablePdu
classes.comment: Section 5.3.12.8: request for data from an entity. COMPLETE

fields.name: requiredReliabilityService
fields.comment: level of reliability service used for this transaction

fields.name: timeInterval
fields.comment: time interval between issuing data query PDUs

classes.name: SetDataReliablePdu
classes.comment: Section 5.3.12.9: initializing or chaning internal state information, reliable. Needs manual intervention to fix     padding on variable datums. UNFINISHED

fields.name: requiredReliabilityService
fields.comment: level of reliability service used for this transaction

classes.name: DataReliablePdu
classes.comment: Section 5.3.12.10: issued in response to a data query R or set dataR pdu. Needs manual intervention      to fix padding on variable datums. UNFINSIHED

fields.name: requiredReliabilityService
fields.comment: level of reliability service used for this transaction

classes.name: EventReportReliablePdu
classes.comment: Section 5.3.12.11: reports the occurance of a significatnt event to the simulation manager. Needs manual     intervention to fix padding in variable datums. UNFINISHED.

classes.name: CommentReliablePdu
classes.comment: Section 5.3.12.12: Arbitrary messages. Only reliable this time. Neds manual intervention     to fix padding in variable datums. UNFINISHED

classes.name: RecordQueryReliablePdu
classes.comment: Section 5.3.12.13: A request for one or more records of data from an entity. COMPLETE

fields.name: requiredReliabilityService
fields.comment: level of reliability service used for this transaction

fields.name: pad1
fields.comment: padding. The spec is unclear and contradictory here.

classes.name: SetRecordReliablePdu
classes.comment: Section 5.3.12.14: Initializing or changing internal parameter info. Needs manual intervention     to fix padding in recrod set PDUs. UNFINISHED

fields.name: requiredReliabilityService
fields.comment: level of reliability service used for this transaction

fields.name: pad1
fields.comment: padding. The spec is unclear and contradictory here.

fields.name: numberOfRecordSets
fields.comment: Number of record sets in list

classes.name: PduContainer
classes.comment: A container that holds PDUs

fields.name: numberOfPdus
fields.comment: Number of PDUs in the container list
